Did you know? Indian Sign Language (ISL) has its own grammar and structure, different from spoken Hindi or English.
Did you know? ISL is used by over a million deaf and hard-of-hearing people in India.
Did you know? Facial expressions are an important part of ISL and can change the meaning of a sign.
Did you know? Sign languages are not universal; each country has its own version.
Did you know? Learning sign language improves visual attention and memory skills.
Did you know? In ISL, word order is often different from English sentence structure.
Did you know? Handshape, movement, and position all contribute to the meaning of a sign.
Did you know? ISL was officially recognized as a language by the Indian government in 2020.
Did you know? Eye contact is essential in sign language communication.
Did you know? Some ISL signs are iconic, meaning they visually resemble what they represent.
Did you know? Finger spelling is used in ISL to spell names or words without signs.
Did you know? Learning sign language can make you more expressive with your body language.
Did you know? ISL does not rely on spoken words; it is completely visual.
Did you know? Signing uses both hands, and sometimes even body posture, to convey meaning.
Did you know? Deaf culture is closely connected to sign language and community identity.
Did you know? In ISL, speed and clarity of movement affect understanding.
Did you know? Practice in front of a mirror can help improve your signing accuracy.
Did you know? Many signs in ISL are context-dependent and can change based on situation.
Did you know? Learning ISL can help bridge communication gaps and promote inclusivity.
Did you know? Even beginners can start communicating basic ideas in ISL within a few days of practice.
